steps.title: Determine what "present the study and its findings" requires
steps.content:
The task is to present both the study (who, when, what was studied) and its findings (what Moreno concluded and why). A choice that omits the conclusion fails the "findings" requirement; a choice that omits the researcher or date fails the "study" requirement.
  • "In 2021, Moreno studied pterosaur jawbones and was initially unsure if the bones belonged to juveniles or adults.": Describes the researcher's initial uncertainty, not the conclusion — no findings presented. ✗
  • "Pterosaur jawbones located in the Sahara Desert were the focus of a 2021 study.": Mentions the study but omits the researcher's name and any findings. ✗
  • "In a 2021 study, Moreno used advanced microscope techniques to analyze the jawbones of pterosaurs, flying reptiles that existed millions of years ago.": Describes methodology only; never states what she concluded. ✗
  • "In a 2021 study, Moreno determined that pterosaur jawbones located in the Sahara Desert had few growth lines relative to the bones of fully grown pterosaurs and thus belonged to juveniles.": Names the researcher and date, states the evidence (few growth lines), and states the conclusion (belonged to juveniles). ✓

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: In a 2021 study, Moreno determined that pterosaur jawbones located in the Sahara Desert had few growth lines relative to the bones of fully grown pterosaurs and thus belonged to juveniles. is the only option that presents both the study (Moreno, 2021) and its findings (jawbones belonged to juveniles, supported by the growth-line evidence). That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
